# Build the IDEA Brand Canvas (purpose → voice)

Setup: Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon pet-care brand. Her dog joint-supplement chew
(ASIN B0PET0005) sits in a crowded, undifferentiated niche — every competitor sells the
same "glucosamine + chondroitin" promise on a beige label. She has an Avatar 2.0 on record
but no brand strategy doc, and wants the eight canvas statements drafted, not a blank worksheet.

---

**Maya (User):** B0PET0005, dog joint-supplement chews. Niche is a sea of sameness — everyone screams "glucosamine + chondroitin, vet-recommended" on the same beige tub. Avatar's done (av_B0PET0005 — worried owners of aging large-breed dogs, US, 35–60, already tried two brands that didn't visibly work). I need the brand canvas: purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value prop, personality, voice. Eight statements. Draft them, don't hand me the worksheet.

**Coach:** On it — eight statements, drafted from your avatar. One guardrail before I draft: the canvas is sequenced **Avatar 2.0 → IDEA Framework → Brand Statements**, and "the order is not optional" — the avatar is the raw material every statement is built from, so I'm loading av_B0PET0005 as the input, not starting from a blank tub.   ⟦tool: build_avatar⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md⟧

⟦tool: build_avatar — input {avatar_id: "av_B0PET0005", mode: "load"}⟧
→ avatar loaded: buyer_intent "find a joint chew that actually visibly helps an aging dog"; motivation "guilt + love — wants more good years, has been burned by products that did nothing"; emotional_triggers ["loss-aversion (running out of time with the dog)","skepticism after two failures","trust"]; shopper_type "Skeptical / been-burned"; demographics "US owners 35–60, large-breed/senior dogs". lock=false, field_source=coach_extracted (server-set).

**Maya:** That's the avatar. Skip the "what is a brand purpose" lecture. The only thing I care about: in a category where every label says the same thing, make the eight statements actually differentiated — and coherent with each other. One brand, not eight random sentences.

**Coach:** That's the exact bar. Two things the skill demands and I'll hold to: (1) keep your statements "coherent with each other, not five disconnected sentences" — one consolidated document, not eight strangers; (2) **Distinctive** is one of the four IDEA principles for a reason — in a crowded market "it is not enough to offer a good product… customers are drawn to brands that feel unique," so every statement has to stand out *for the right reasons*, not just describe a chew. I need one input to ground all eight: your "why" beyond selling the chew. One sentence, then I draft.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/02-brand/01-brand-canvas/01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/02-idea-framework/00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md⟧

**Maya:** Why I built it: I watched my own lab go stiff at 9 and tried four supplements that did nothing visible. I got obsessed with what actually moves the needle — therapeutic dose, third-party tested, not the underdosed sprinkle most brands sell. So: clinically-meaningful doses, lab-verified, results you can actually see in your dog — for owners who've already been let down. That's it. Draft it.

**Coach:** Pulling the structured brand-foundation context from that so every statement traces to your own words, not a category template.   ⟦tool: run_conversation — input {message: "<Maya's founding-why + brand promise>", avatar_context: "av_B0PET0005 dog joint chews"}⟧

⟦tool: run_conversation⟧
→ extracted_context: { founding_why: "watched own senior dog decline; burned by underdosed supplements that did nothing", core_promise: "clinically-meaningful doses, third-party lab-verified, visible results for aging dogs", emotional_spine: "trust earned back after disappointment", category: "premium senior-dog joint supplements", differentiation_axis: "therapeutic dose + proof vs underdosed sprinkle" }

**Coach:** Context captured. The whole canvas runs against one lens — the four IDEA principles: **Insight-Driven** (address needs beyond the product), **Distinctive** (stand out in a busy market for the right reasons), **Empathetic** (connect on a human level), **Authentic** (honesty, consistency, deliver on promises). Each statement below is checked against all four — because in your category most brands fail Distinctive and Authentic, and a brand here is "a relationship between you and your customers," not just a label. Exporting the canvas now.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/02-idea-framework/00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md⟧

⟦tool: export_strategy_doc — input {avatar_id: "av_B0PET0005", brand_context: "<extracted_context>", section: "brand_canvas_statements", format: "doc"}⟧
→ strategy doc generated: 8 brand statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value_proposition, personality, voice); idempotent per avatar snapshot.
⟦iv-os: log_asset — brand-canvas doc linked to B0PET0005⟧ *(mocked: no canon/asset/claims data invented by coach)*

**Coach:** Eight statements, drafted and coherent — purpose is present-facing, vision future-facing, mission is what you *do*, voice expresses the personality. The spine that makes them differentiated: **earned trust through proven, therapeutic dosing** — not "vet-recommended" wallpaper. All edit-ready below.

### Artifact produced

**IDEA Brand Canvas — B0PET0005 (senior-dog joint chews) — DRAFT, edit-ready**

| # | Statement | Your statement (drafted for you) | IDEA check + grounding |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Brand Purpose** *(the "why" beyond selling products — present-facing)* | "To give aging dogs more good years by making joint care that actually works — clinically-meaningful doses, proven and visible — so owners who've been let down once never have to settle again." | Names a need beyond the chew: more good years (Insight); claims a higher mission than the underdosed category (Distinctive); resonates with the guilt/love of a worried owner (Empathetic); rings true to why you started after your own dog (Authentic). Held to the sample's brevity. — `02-foundational-statements/00-brand-purpose.md` |
| 2 | **Brand Vision** *(aspirational future impact — future-facing)* | "To become the joint supplement skeptical owners trust by default — the brand that ended the era of beige tubs and 'sprinkle-and-hope' dosing in pet wellness." | Genuinely aspirational and category-redefining, not a restated purpose; sets a unique direction (Distinctive) and stays anchored to a commitment you can actually honor (Authentic). — `02-foundational-statements/01-brand-vision.md` |
| 3 | **Brand Mission** *(the actionable steps that fulfill purpose + vision — what you DO)* | "To formulate joint chews at therapeutic, label-honest doses, third-party test every batch, and publish the proof — so owners of aging dogs can see results, not just read claims." | Reads as something the brand *does*; names the action (formulate at therapeutic dose), the beneficiary (owners of aging dogs), and the without-compromise promise (proof, not claims) like the worked sample. — `02-foundational-statements/02-brand-mission.md` |
| 4 | **Brand Values** *(guiding principles — credible only when shown in action)* | "Efficacy, transparency, honesty, and devotion. We dose to what the research says works, we publish every batch's lab results, we refuse to claim what we can't show, and we treat your dog's last years like we treated ours." | Each value paired with a concrete commitment (the Authentic test = actions reflect promises), not a bare adjective list. — `02-foundational-statements/03-brand-values.md` |
| 5 | **Positioning Statement** *(how you stand out + why you're the best choice — "For [audience]…" shape)* | "For owners of aging dogs who've already tried supplements that did nothing, our chews deliver clinically-meaningful, lab-verified doses you can see working — so they can stop guessing and start giving their dog the comfort they deserve." | Follows the For-audience / offering / differentiator / emotional-payoff structure; differentiates rather than describes — the exact failure mode of the beige-tub category. Audience pulled from av_B0PET0005. — `03-positioning-statements/00-positioning-statement.md` |
| 6 | **Value Proposition** *(why buy from you vs a competitor — practical AND emotional)* | "Therapeutic doses, third-party-tested batches, and results you can actually watch in your dog — the joint chew that earns back the trust the last one lost." | Stacks practical value (dose, testing, visible results) AND lands an emotional payoff (earns back lost trust) — passes both halves of the Empathetic test. — `03-positioning-statements/01-value-proposition.md` |
| 7 | **Brand Personality** *(human-like traits + an archetype — the input to voice)* | "Honest, rigorous, warm, and protective. Your brand is the straight-talking vet-friend who shows the lab results — never the hype-man, never the cartoon paw-print mascot." | Names traits AND an archetype/role ("the straight-talking vet-friend who shows the lab results") so it's usable, not just adjectives; feeds directly into voice. — `04-expression-statements/00-brand-personality.md` |
| 8 | **Brand Voice** *(tone, language, style across every platform — expresses the personality)* | "Plain-spoken, evidence-first, and tender about the dog. We state the dose, then the proof — and we'd answer a worried 'will this actually help my dog?' email in the same honest voice we write the headline." | Gives a tone trio AND a how-we-speak line; passes the Authentic operational test (must hold up in a support reply, not just a hero headline). Written after personality is settled. — `04-expression-statements/01-brand-voice.md` |

**Coherence note (canvas as one document):** purpose ("more good years, actually works") → vision ("ended the era of beige tubs") → mission ("therapeutic dose, publish the proof") → values ("efficacy, transparency") → positioning/value-prop ("clinically-meaningful, lab-verified, visible") → personality ("shows the lab results") → voice ("dose, then proof") all run on one spine: **earned trust through proven, therapeutic dosing.** That single spine is what makes the canvas *Distinctive* in a same-y category — and it's the single source of truth you'd feed a BrandGPT later.

**One next action:** Edit any of the eight (start with Values — name one concrete way the brand already demonstrates each; if you can't show it, that value is aspirational, not real, per the Authentic test). Send edits and I'll regenerate the doc against them.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)

- [tool-call] `build_avatar` invoked first with `{avatar_id:"av_B0PET0005", mode:"load"}` to satisfy the canvas sequencing rule (avatar is the input before statements).
- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked once to extract structured brand-foundation context (founding-why + core promise + differentiation axis) from Maya's free-text.
- [tool-call] `export_strategy_doc` invoked with `{avatar_id:"av_B0PET0005", section:"brand_canvas_statements", format:"doc"}`; returned 8 statements; only these 3 tools called (no off-journey tools).
- [schema] `export_strategy_doc` output contains exactly the 8 canvas statements (purpose, vision, mission, values, positioning, value_proposition, personality, voice); `build_avatar` load returns avatar fields + lock state + server-set `field_source`.
- [skill-faithful] The "Avatar 2.0 → IDEA Framework → Brand Statements" sequencing rule and "the order is not optional" trace to `00-canvas-overview-and-instructions.md`.
- [skill-faithful] "keep your statements coherent with each other, not disconnected sentences" / one consolidated document traces to `01-the-canvas-tables/02-brand-statements-canvas-table.md`.
- [skill-faithful] The four-principle lens (Insight-Driven / Distinctive / Empathetic / Authentic), "a brand is a relationship, not just an identity," and the Distinctive emphasis ("in a crowded marketplace… customers are drawn to brands that feel unique and memorable") trace to `00-overview-and-why-trust-wins/02-the-four-principles.md` — load-bearing here because the niche is undifferentiated.
- [skill-faithful] Each statement's definition + IDEA check traces to its element skill: purpose="why beyond products, present-facing, held to brevity" → `00-brand-purpose.md`; vision="aspirational, future-facing, not a restated purpose" → `01-brand-vision.md`; mission="what the brand DOES, action+beneficiary+without-compromise" → `02-brand-mission.md`; values="credible only when shown in action" → `03-brand-values.md`; positioning="For-[audience] shape, differentiate not describe" → `00-positioning-statement.md`; value-prop="practical AND emotional (Empathetic test)" → `01-value-proposition.md`; personality="traits + archetype, input to voice" → `00-brand-personality.md`; voice="tone trio + how-we-speak, holds up in a support reply" → `01-brand-voice.md`.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Compression + done-for-you: coach drafted all 8 statements FROM her avatar + founding-why (no blank worksheet handed back), led with the answer + one-line why, skipped the offered "brand-purpose lecture," batched the canvas into one table, ended with exactly one next action. ≤1 input request (the founding-why, which is required raw material per the sequencing rule). Maya's terse, jargon-correct, data-dropping pushback ("make them actually differentiated") is answered with the Distinctive principle, not a definition.
- [artifact] A fully drafted 8-statement IDEA Brand Canvas (purpose → voice) is present, each statement edit-ready and grounded, with a coherence spine that addresses the crowded-niche brief.
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; no invented reviews/stats/clinical claims/canon — IV-OS `log_asset` shown as an `⟦iv-os:⟧` mock, never invented as real data; the avatar input is the user's own on-record av_B0PET0005, server-set `field_source` preserved. "Third-party tested / therapeutic dose" appear as brand-positioning language the owner supplied, not as substantiated claims the coach asserts.
